embeds.title: Echo Generation 2
embeds.description: **Echo Generation 2** is a turn\-based sci\-fi deckbuilding RPG from the creators of the award\-winning Echo Generation \(Best Indie Game, Best Narrative, Best Soundtrack\)\.…

content: Ye it feels like actual card game and the roguelike aspect isnt tedious and adds more depth with strategizing on what relic to choose which could make a mediocre deck into a good, as well the purge card mechanic

prologue

that one

so the tldr is pretty simple: we are investigators in our "graduating" ceremony where we receive the "silver key": a VERY important item that allows us to resonate with awakeners and fight dissolution.

Sadly, something goes very awry, and every single person in the class gets dissoluted and turned into monsters, thus, with the help of our companions (ramona, ogier and lotan) we have to fight our way through the core of the university (the black pool) so we can use our silver key to stabilize everything again
the mc is kinda special, not all investigators are able to hold a silver key, the ones which can are called "keepers"
(as in keepers of the silver key, or keepers of secrets)

i mean in an example:
1. boss applies paint
2. saya applies warp on the same card 

in such a situation, would the 2 effects stack or overwrite?
hmm
if it stacks, you could end up with edge cases where a card costs +-4 instead of +-2
can they just code it as only 1 effect can exist on the card
that would be overwriting. which could work, but i assume the intention is to prio cards that are normal first, which overwriting will dampen the effect of
since less cards in total will be affected
